metadata.text:
# AC Maintenance Schedule — Residential Customers

## How often should I service my central AC?

Carrier and Trane units in hot climates (90°F+ summer design) should receive **professional maintenance twice per year**:

1. **Spring tune-up** (March–April): refrigerant check, coil cleaning, capacitor test, thermostat calibration.
2. **Fall inspection** (September–October): heat mode verification for heat pumps, defrost cycle test, filter housing check.

## Warning signs between visits
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/ac-maintenance-schedule.md

metadata.text:
## Warning signs between visits

- Warm air from supply vents when cooling mode is selected
- Ice on the outdoor condenser line set
- Short cycling (unit turns on/off every 2–3 minutes)
- Unusual grinding or squealing from the blower motor

## Filter replacement

Replace 1-inch pleated filters every **60–90 days**. MERV 11–13 is recommended for homes with pets or allergy sufferers. Never run the system without a filter installed.

## Priority dispatch
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/ac-maintenance-schedule.md

metadata.text:
## Priority dispatch

Customers reporting **no cooling** during heat advisories qualify for **P1 emergency dispatch** when outdoor temperatures exceed 95°F and the home has vulnerable occupants (elderly, infants, medical equipment).
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/ac-maintenance-schedule.md

metadata.text:
# Furnace No-Heat Troubleshooting Guide

## Quick checks before calling for service

1. **Thermostat**: Confirm mode is HEAT, setpoint is above room temperature, and batteries are fresh (if battery-powered).
2. **Power**: Verify the furnace switch (looks like a light switch near the unit) is ON. Check the breaker panel for a tripped breaker labeled HVAC or Furnace.
3. **Filter**: A severely clogged filter can trip high-limit safety switches. Replace if dirty.

## When to schedule a technician
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/furnace-no-heat-troubleshooting.md

metadata.text:
## When to schedule a technician

| Symptom | Likely cause | Recommended priority |
|---------|--------------|---------------------|
| Blower runs, no warm air | Ignitor or flame sensor | P2 (next-day) |
| Clicking but no ignition | Failed hot surface ignitor | P2 |
| Burning smell on first start | Dust burn-off (normal once) | Monitor |
| Gas odor | Possible leak — **evacuate and call gas utility** | P1 emergency |

## Warranty note
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/furnace-no-heat-troubleshooting.md

metadata.text:
## Warranty note

Units under **annual maintenance contract** receive no diagnostic fee for no-heat calls placed before 10 AM on weekdays.
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/furnace-no-heat-troubleshooting.md

metadata.text:
# Heat Pump Ice Build-Up in Winter

## Is ice on the outdoor unit normal?

Light frost on the outdoor coil during defrost cycles is **normal** for air-source heat pumps operating below 40°F ambient. However, **thick ice encasing the unit** is not normal and indicates:

- Restricted airflow (blocked coil, snow drift against unit)
- Low refrigerant charge
- Failed defrost board or sensor
- Stuck reversing valve

## What the customer can do safely
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/heat-pump-ice-in-winter.md

metadata.text:
## What the customer can do safely

- Clear snow and debris in a **2-foot perimeter** around the outdoor unit
- Do **not** chip ice with tools — coil fins bend easily
- Switch to auxiliary/emergency heat on the thermostat temporarily if indoor temperature drops below 65°F

## Service recommendation
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/heat-pump-ice-in-winter.md

metadata.text:
## Service recommendation

Schedule **P2 urgent** service when ice persists more than 4 hours or indoor temperature falls more than 5°F below setpoint. Document recurrence — third occurrence within 90 days may indicate warranty claim eligibility on qualifying Lennox and Carrier models.
metadata.source: /Users/danielvercueil/Desktop/HVAC Inbound Voice Agent and Churn Predictor Project/data/knowledge/faqs/heat-pump-ice-in-winter.md
